skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem:
Data set X: 7, 11, 11, 15

Data set Y: 7, 11, 11, 15, 1

The lists give the values in data sets X and Y. Which statement correctly compares the mean of data set X and the mean of data set Y?

answerOptions.content: The mean of data set X is less than the mean of data set Y.
The means of data set X and data set Y are equal.
There is not enough information to compare the means.
The mean of data set X is greater than the mean of data set Y.

rationale:
Choice D is correct. The mean of data set X is (7 + 11 + 11 + 15) divided by 4 = 44 divided by 4 = 11. The mean of data set Y is (7 + 11 + 11 + 15 + 1) divided by 5 = 45 divided by 5 = 9. Since 11 is greater than 9, the mean of X is greater than the mean of Y.

Choices A, B, and C are inconsistent with the computed means.
